news.title: Spain wins the 2026 World Cup
news.lede: Welcome to the final episode of the Daily Cup! Hosts Tamerra Griffin and James McNicholas break down the epic conclusion to the 2026 World Cup, where Spain emerged victorious. Felipe Cardenas joins the desk to analyze the dramatic 120-minute final and Lionel Messi’s future. Then, Hannah Vanbiber shares the ultimate cheat sheet on how to follow your favorite stars like Erling Haaland and Kylian Mbappe as they return to their club teams.

news.title: Gaza celebrates World Cup victory of Spanish team they took into their hearts
news.lede: People gather at cafes and outdoor screenings to support team after Spain’s backing for Palestinian rights. The Spanish football team’s World Cup win has been celebrated in the streets of Gaza, where Spain’s support for Palestinian rights and Israel’s backing for the Argentinian team turned the competition into more than a football showdown. Gathered at cafes and outdoor screenings, Palestinians defied the risk of Israeli attacks to cheer on a team they had taken to their hearts. Many waving Spanish flags paid tribute to teenage star Lamine Yamal, who brought a Palestinian flag to celebrations for Barcelona’s La Liga win in May.

news.title: 2026 World Cup: All the European sides' results
news.lede: Look back at all the European sides' results at the FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States. The 2026 FIFA World Cup in Canada, Mexico and the United States kicked off on 11 June and concluded with Spain's extra-time final victory against holders Argentina in East Rutherford on 19 July. Check out all the details of the European teams' matches. Wednesday 17 June: W 3-1 vs Jordan (San Francisco Bay Area) Monday 22 June: L 0-2 vs Argentina (Dallas) Sunday 28 June: D 3-3 vs Algeria (Kansas City) Thursday 2 July (R32): L 3-0 vs Spain (Los Angeles).

news.title: World Cup becoming a 64-team tournament would create logistical challenges - so can Gianni Infantino's idea work?
news.lede: This year's World Cup is finally over - and after 48 teams took part, it was a long, long tournament. But just as we bid to say goodbye to the 2026 edition, this competition may be getting even bigger. FIFA president Gianni Infantino says any further expansion of the World Cup for 2030 - where six countries are set to host the tournament - ‌will be discussed now the 2026 World Cup has finished. And one solution FIFA are discussing is a 64-team tournament.

news.title: Donald Trump’s big World Cup day out: crowd boos, player snubs and failed photobomb
news.lede: At the final moment, Donald Trump finally made his World Cup entrance. And it wasn’t all smooth sailing. The US president, who has kept largely out of the limelight over the last five weeks, made his major intervention a fortnight ago after USA striker Folarin Balogun had his red card ban suspended, before their last-16 exit to Belgium, after some lobbying from Trump and the US administration. Trump did take part in a press conference with Fifa president Gianni Infantino on Friday and was always expected to attend the final at the New York New Jersey Stadium on Sunday between Spain and Argentina.

news.title: Nico Williams, Ferran Torres, and Spain’s adaptable World Cup excellence
news.lede: The two substitutes combined on the World Cup winning goal not by changing the game, but by offering it what it needed, when it was needed. As Spain built into the second half of extra-time of Sunday’s World Cup final, it was already clear that Argentina hoped to make them suffer. Every challenge left no stud unshown. Their defenders labored to prevent striker Mikel Oyarzabal from his typical lanes of service, with Spain taking eleven of their 20 attempts from beyond the box.

news.title: The World Cup may be over, but the other football is just around the corner: When does the NFL preseason start
news.lede: The whole world has been wrapped up in a soccer frenzy, but especially in North America, where the World Cup was hosted. With Spain’s thrilling victory over defending champions Argentina in New Jersey, the tournament has now reached its end. The confetti has settled and the country is feeling those same, familiar post-sports blues that come with the end of each season. Thankfully, it won’t last long this time, as the NFL season is just around the corner.

news.title: 'Kick Argentina out of the World Cup' petition nearly breaks Guinness World Record
news.lede: What a fun World Cup this has been... If only FIFA and Argentina hadn't ruined the whole thing. It's a sentiment shared by many online, who believe that this year's tournament has brought the culture of football further into disrepute - whether it's the exorbitant ticket prices, the controversial "hydration breaks", the Trump-Infantino relationship leading to the infamous red card scandal, some dubious refereeing and allegations of favouritism... The list goes on, and went on up until the final, in which Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time.
